_meta.description: App-wide read-only seed bank of LinkedIn CTA patterns, comment-gating templates, and P.S. closers for the Choir content-OS. Goal-mapped so the post-composer can select a CTA by the member's stated objective (lead-gen, engagement, follow, DM, booking). Templates contain {placeholder} tokens an engineer/LLM fills at generation time.
_meta.placeholder_convention: Square-bracket tokens [like_this] and curly tokens {like_this} are fill-at-generation slots. Preserve them verbatim in the DB; resolve at render.

design_rules.detail: Multiple CTAs dilute action. Composer must emit exactly one cta_pattern per generated post.
'Comment your biggest hiring mistake' beats 'Let me know what you think'.
'Drop a [emoji]' is easier than 'Write a paragraph'.
frameworks = save, opinions = comment, personal brand = follow.
Always consider 'is this saveable?' when writing.

design_rules.rule: CTA is worth ~10% of writing time
design_rules.detail: Time allocation: 40% hook/rehook, 30% body, 20% editing/formatting, 10% CTA.
Posts without CTAs get up to 40% less engagement than posts with CTAs. Ending with a question can increase engagement by 20-40% (Richard van der Blom, Algorithm Insights 2024).

cta_patterns.when_to_use: Goal = maximize reach. Best for framework, checklist, and reference posts. Saves are weighted ~5x by the algorithm — pair with saveable content.

cta_patterns.template: Bookmark this for next time you [situation]. You'll thank yourself.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: High-performing save-driving variant. Use on evergreen tactical/how-to posts the reader will want to return to.

cta_patterns.template: Save this framework for your next [moment, e.g. pipeline review]. Repost if your team needs it too.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: Framework/system posts where the goal is saves plus organic amplification. Example given for a sales-framework post.

cta_patterns.template: What's your #1 challenge with [topic]? Drop it below.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: Goal = boost engagement. Best for opinion posts and discussion starters. Specific ask outperforms 'let me know what you think'.

cta_patterns.template: I'm curious -- what would you add to this list? Drop your #1 in the comments.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: High-performing comment-driving variant. Use on list/listicle posts to invite additions and lower the barrier (one item, not a paragraph).

cta_patterns.template: Agree or disagree? I'll respond to every comment.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: Debate-driving. Best for contrarian takes and opinion posts. The promise to reply to every comment boosts dwell time and comment volume.

cta_patterns.template: Controversial? Maybe. But I've seen it play out [N]+ times. What's your experience -- does [X] matter more than [Y]? Tell me below.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: Contrarian/opinion posts (e.g. hiring takes). Anchors authority with a count, then opens a binary debate to drive comments.

cta_patterns.template: What's been your experience with this?
cta_patterns.when_to_use: Generic question close from the 8-part winning post structure. Ending a post with a question can increase engagement by 20-40%. Use when no more specific prompt fits.

cta_patterns.template: Drop a comment if you agree.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: Low-barrier agreement prompt from the 8-part structure example CTAs. Use for values/opinion posts that want quick directional engagement.
Minimal save prompt from the 8-part structure example CTAs. Use as a short tail on dense value posts.

cta_patterns.template: I share [topic] frameworks weekly. Follow for more.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: Goal = grow followers. Best for personal-brand posts and consistent-niche creators. Signals a recurring value reason to follow.

cta_patterns.template: I break down [niche] strategies like this every week. Follow along.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: High-performing follow-driving variant. Use after a strong breakdown/teardown post to convert one-time readers into followers.

cta_patterns.template: DM me '[keyword]' and I'll send you the template.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: Goal = generate leads. Best when you have a gated asset (template/checklist/framework). Moves the reader into a 1:1 channel.

cta_patterns.template: Want the full [template/checklist/framework]? DM me '[keyword]' and I'll send it over.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: High-performing DM-driving variant. Use when teasing a complete resource only partially shown in the post.

cta_patterns.template: I wrote a full guide on this. Link in comments.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: Goal = build email list / drive off-platform clicks. Keeps the link out of the post body (LinkedIn deprioritizes outbound links in-body) by routing it to the first comment.

cta_patterns.template: If you're not measuring [thing], you're waiting for [the bad outcome]. Save this post for the framework I used.
cta_patterns.when_to_use: AIDA 'Action' close that pairs loss framing with a save-for-the-asset prompt. Use on case-study/transformation posts where the framework is the lead hook.

cta_patterns.template: [CTA - exact next step: DM, email, book a call]
cta_patterns.when_to_use: Profile About-section closer (not a post CTA). State the exact next step to convert profile visitors into booked calls/leads. The profile is a landing page — every element must convert.
Profile headline (220 chars max) that doubles as a booking CTA via social proof. Used in the B2B founder profile-rewrite example. Avoid 'visionary/guru/ninja/thought leader' or job title alone.

comment_gating.pattern: Comment "[KEYWORD]" below and I'll share it.
comment_gating.when_to_use: Highest-engagement CTA. Comment-gated posts drive 3-5x normal comment volume; the algorithm sees massive comments and boosts distribution. Use the keyword as the trigger to DM the gated asset.

comment_gating.pattern: Comment "STACK" and I'll send you the full breakdown.
comment_gating.when_to_use: Verbatim proven gate (ColdIQ). 'STACK' for tech-stack / tool-breakdown posts where the full breakdown is the gated deliverable.

comment_gating.pattern: Comment "[KEYWORD]" and make sure we're connected so I can DM it to you.
comment_gating.when_to_use: Combines comment-gate + connection request so the DM can actually land (LinkedIn limits DMs to connections). Example keyword shown: 'CLAUDE'. Use when the asset must be delivered via DM.

comment_gating.pattern: Want the template? Drop a 🔥 below.
comment_gating.when_to_use: Lowest-barrier comment gate (single emoji, no typing). Maximizes comment count for distribution; pair with DM follow-up to deliver the asset.

ps_closers.pattern: P.S: What tool should I add to this stack?
ps_closers.when_to_use: Proven highest-performing closer. Best for tech-stack / tool-reveal posts. Invites the reader to contribute, driving comments.

ps_closers.pattern: P.S: How are your cold email campaigns doing right now?
ps_closers.when_to_use: Proven closer for outbound / cold-email content. Opens a relatable status-check conversation.

ps_closers.pattern: P.S: What's the ONE tool you can't live without?
ps_closers.when_to_use: Proven closer for tooling / productivity posts. The 'ONE' constraint lowers the barrier and sharpens replies.

ps_closers.pattern: P.S: Which part of this system would you want more detail on?
ps_closers.when_to_use: Proven closer for system / playbook posts. Doubles as content-research — surfaces what to write next.

continuation_tease.pattern: I'm running out of space... Continuing in the comments 👇
continuation_tease.when_to_use: Drives the reader into the comments (boosting dwell time + comment thread depth) by teasing that the post continues there. Use on long posts that hit the character cap.

continuation_tease.pattern: Make sure to follow so you don't miss the next breakdown.
continuation_tease.when_to_use: Follow tease for serialized / recurring breakdown content. Pair with a continuation tease or end of a multi-part post.
